sutta: the buddha said monks i will teach you the unworthy man and the still more unworthy man i will teach you the worthy man and the still more worthy men do you listen carefully apply your minds and i will speak yes lord replied those monks to the exalted 1 the exalted 1 said and of what sword monks is the unworthy man herein a certain person is 1 who takes life steals is an adulterer is a liar is given to the use of liquor fermented and distilled causing negligence this 1 is called the unworthy man and of what sort monks is the still more unworthy man hearing a certain person is 1 who takes life steels etc and further encourages another to do the same this 1 is called the still more unworthy man and of what sword monks is the worthy man hearing a certain 1 is 1 who abstains from taking life from stealing etc this 1 is called the worthy man and of what sword monks is a still more worthy man hearing a certain person not only himself abstains from taking life from stealing etc but also encourages another to abstain from doing so this 1 is called the still more worthy man that's the end of the sutta
commentary: in this uttar the buddha is saying that a person who breaks the 5 precepts is an unworthy person who kills steals commits adultery lies takes fermented liquor and he's even worse now if he encourages another to do so and likewise with the worthy men now later on we will come across another suta for 261 to 267 where the buddha says if a person does evil and on top of that he encourages others to do evil and furthermore approves of doing evil and even praises the doing of evil then that person can go to the woeful plains and even hell so if we do good and we encourage others to do good and then we get still more married whereas if we do evil and encourage others to do evil and then our demerit or our evil is multiplied

sutta: the buddha said amongst these 4 states conduced to growth in wisdom what for association with a true man hearing such dharma or true dharma thorough consideration or proper consideration and behavior in accordance with dhamma these are the 4 monks these 4 states are of great service to 1 who has become human what for association with a true man hearing the true dhamma terror consideration and behavior in accordance with dhamma these are the 4 that's the end of the suta
commentary: so here you can hear the buddha saying that these 4 things will bring us wisdom and also they would be a great service to human beings and the first 1 is to associate with a true man 1 who knows the dhamma 1 who knows the true dharma and can guide you along the path of the true dharma the second 1 is hearing the true dharma the true dhamma here meaning the discourses of the buddha the original discourses of the buddha in our 4 nikayas third 1 is thorough consideration or proper consideration when we hear we have to consider properly but this thorough consideration or proper consideration is possible if you have a very clear mind if you have samadhi if you have a some strength of mind your mind is not too scattered then only you have this yoniso proper consideration and in this case meditation helps a lot meditation helps us to control our mind to strengthen our mind so that we can consider right to the source of the problem uniso manasi kara means considering the problem up to the source and the last 1 is behavior in accordance with dhamma this 1 is very important whatever we learn in the dharma we must behave in accordance with the dhamma there's no point to hear dhamma let it go in 1 ear and go out the other ear and then it's of no benefit to us we must put into practice so these 4 things should remember very important first 1 association with the true man second 1 hearing the true dhamma third 1 proper consideration or thorough consideration fourth 1 behavior in accordance with dhamma
